name: Pica Lulū
description: Major Latvian pizza chain operating 24/7 with its own ordering, app and in-house delivery, focused on consumers in Riga and nearby towns and on business/catering orders.

details.business: B2C restaurant & delivery (plus B2B catering/office orders)
details.targetMarket: Retail consumers in Riga, Jurmala and nearby towns (late-night and convenience delivery), plus corporate/event catering customers

items: Mobile app (iOS) for faster repeat orders
49-minute delivery guarantee (free pizza if delivery >49 minutes, limited conditions)
Wide pizza menu (meat, fish, vegetarian, vegan, calzone)
Large-format pizzas (45cm options shown on third-party listings)
Desserts, snacks, drinks and gift cards
Multiple pizzerias (~19 locations mentioned on site/news), 24/7 service in many locations
Franchise / partner opportunities mentioned on LinkedIn and site pages

missingFeatures: No public, real-time courier tracking or map-based ETA — deliveries are confirmed via operator call (terms state delivery is considered complete when operator calls), creating UX friction and higher perceived uncertainty.
No visible loyalty/subscription program on the website (search for loyalty/lojalitāte returns no results), limiting repeat-customer retention opportunities and predictable revenue from subscriptions.

Corporate ordering lacks a self-serve portal or API — business customers are asked to email a contact rather than use an online bulk-ordering or invoiced account, creating friction for large/recurring corporate orders.

Delivery guarantee is narrowly packaged (applies only to orders of not more than two pizzas and can be suspended), which makes SLA guarantees confusing for larger or party orders — an opportunity for clearer, more flexible delivery SLAs or premium on-time options.

Limited publicly-documented delivery coverage and scheduling options (guarantee zones and exclusions are noted but there is no interactive coverage map or scheduled-preorder portal), reducing conversion for customers outside core zones or who want future-time deliveries.

pricingPlans.name: Menu (single order)
pricingPlans.note: Entry-level pizza price shown on the online menu (many pizzas start from €8.49)

pricingPlans.name: Corporate / catering
pricingPlans.note: Business / event orders handled via direct contact (email) — no public self-serve pricing

pricingEvidence.label: Menu page
pricingEvidence.claim: Many pizzas listed with prices, several starting from €8.49.

pricingEvidence.label: Terms of delivery
pricingEvidence.claim: Minimum order and delivery fee are stated (example: minimum €8.99 + €5.00 delivery fee within 49-minute zone).

companyEvidence.label: About page (Pica Lulū)
companyEvidence.claim: States the brand has been operating since 1993/1994 and positions itself as the largest pizza roller since 1994.

companyEvidence.label: Cooperation / News page
companyEvidence.claim: SIA Later LTD (Pica Lulū) described as the largest pizza producer in Latvia with more than 360 employees and operating since 1994.

companyEvidence.label: Company registry (Lursoft / company record for Later Ltd)
companyEvidence.claim: Legal entity SIA Later LTD registered (reg. No. 40003119373) with company details; public company records show founding/registration information.

reviewEvidence.label: Tripadvisor review
reviewEvidence.claim: Several guest reviews mention late deliveries and long waits; one review complained about delayed orders and referenced the 49-minute free-pizza policy.

reviewEvidence.label: Reddit discussion (r/latvia)
reviewEvidence.claim: Long-running community complaints about delivery delays, difficulty contacting the chain, and inconsistent courier reliability.

reviewEvidence.label: RestaurantGuru listing
reviewEvidence.claim: Aggregate ratings and reviews show mixed sentiment and note delivery/taste/service variability.

featureEvidence.label: Delivery guarantee (news/terms)
featureEvidence.claim: Public promise to deliver within 49 minutes or the pizza is free (with conditions).

featureEvidence.label: Terms of delivery
featureEvidence.claim: Describes delivery zones, minimum order amounts, and the mechanics of the delivery guarantee (including limitations).

featureEvidence.label: Mobile app listing (App Store) / marketplace presence
featureEvidence.claim: Pica Lulū offers a mobile app to place orders (store listing indicating app availability).

strengths: Strong local brand with long track record (operating since early 1990s) — trusted name in Riga and surrounding areas.
Operational focus on in-house delivery and 24/7 availability, supported by a public 49-minute delivery guarantee that drives conversion for time-sensitive customers.
Extensive menu and customization (build-your-own pizzas, vegetarian/vegan options, large-format pizzas) and multiple ordering channels (site, app, phone, marketplace).
weaknesses: Frequent customer complaints about late or cold deliveries and difficulty contacting support (multiple reviews and community posts cite delivery reliability issues).
Low digital transparency: no public real-time tracking of couriers and order completion is tied to an operator call, which hurts UX and perceived reliability.
Limited self-serve B2B tooling — corporate customers must email a contact rather than use a dedicated corporate portal, creating friction for large or repeat orders.
Delivery guarantee limitations (applies to ≤2 pizzas and can be suspended) create confusing expectations for group/party orders and reduce the appeal of guarantees for higher-value purchases.
